source_title: CA FAIR Plan Rate Increase 2026: What Homeowners Must Know
subject: A European angle on your CA FAIR Plan Rate Increase 2026: What Homeowners Must Know piece
body:
Hi Mariah,

Your breakdown of the CA FAIR Plan rate increases was genuinely useful — the way you walked through what the changes mean for everyday homeowners, not just the policy mechanics, made a complicated subject feel approachable and real.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete (cemento a vista), a construction method long favored across the Mediterranean for its resilience and longevity. Given how much insurability now hinges on structural integrity and fire resistance, that building tradition feels increasingly relevant to conversations like the one you started.

Would you be open to receiving more background on the project, or a brief call with our founder Paolo Mezzalama? No pressure either way — happy to share materials first if that's easier.

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

source_title: California FAIR Plan to raise homeowners insurance rates about 29%
subject: A European angle on your California FAIR Plan to raise homeowners insurance rates abo piece
body:
Hi Alexis,

Your piece on the California FAIR Plan rate hike was a clear-eyed look at how dramatically the coverage landscape is shifting for homeowners across the state — particularly for those in higher-risk areas who are already running out of options.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete (cemento a vista), a material tradition rooted in Mediterranean climate-adaptive architecture. There's a meaningful connection here: construction method increasingly factors into how insurers assess risk and price policies.

If any of this feels relevant to a future story, I'd love to send over more background — or arrange a brief conversation with Paolo Mezzalama, our founder, if that would be useful.

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

source_title: California Builders Embrace Fire-Resistant Construction Amid Rising Risks - Davis Vanguard
subject: A European angle on your California Builders Embrace Fire-Resistant Construction Amid piece
body:
Hi David,

Your piece on fire-resistant ADU construction in San Jose caught my attention — the way you traced how builders are rethinking materials from the ground up, not just retrofitting, felt like a genuinely different angle on a conversation that usually stays surface-level.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete — cemento a vista — a Mediterranean tradition where structural resilience and aesthetic intention have always been the same thing. That philosophy maps closely onto what California builders are now being pushed toward by necessity.

Paolo Mezzalama, our founder, would love to share how this approach translates practically in an LA context. Would you be open to a short call or receiving some background materials?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

publication: Robb Report Shelter | Luxury Real Estate and Interior Design
source_title: Hollywood Scion Jesse Bochco’s Custom-Built L.A. Home Lists for $15 Million
subject: A European angle on your Hollywood Scion Jesse Bochco’s Custom-Built L.A. Home Lists  piece
body:
Hi Wendy,

Your piece on Jesse Bochco's Venice home was a genuinely enjoyable read — the way you traced the design decisions back to his personal history made the architecture feel like biography, which is rare in property coverage.

My Villa is an LA-based developer building Italian-designed homes in reinforced exposed concrete — cemento a vista — bringing a Mediterranean material tradition that prizes permanence and texture over trend. That sensibility feels like a natural conversation partner with the kind of intentional, story-driven builds your readers gravitate toward.

Paolo Mezzalama, our founder, would love to connect if you're open to it — a short call or some background materials, whatever works best for you.

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la
